The Tactics
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 161 --

“WEAPONIZED COMMUNITY”?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

2) Allow price premiums (PROFIT)

virality (⬇ CPA) (TOP OF MIND)

-- 17 of 161 --

All Great Companies/Cultures
Are Based on STORIES
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 18 of 161 --

Stories Make People Enter a Different
Cognitive State
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 19 of 161 --

It makes them more suggestible, which
can be used to transform someone’s
life & break beliefs, or ruin them.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 20 of 161 --

Knowing how to do this is one of THE
MOST POWERFUL skills you can
learn.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 21 of 161 --

Because it gives you INFLUENCE...www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 22 of 161 --

And since we can accomplish NOTHING
great on our own, we need to learn to
convince others to JOIN US....
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 27 of 161 --

First, you need to know how to actually
tell a story...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 28 of 161 --

Know anyone who sucks at telling
jokes?
Or says “You had to be there”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 29 of 161 --

It means they don’t have the skillset to
TAKE you there....
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 30 of 161 --

And make you FEEL, what was FELT.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 31 of 161 --

Storytelling Made Simple:
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 32 of 161 --

Storytelling Made Simple:
1) Backstory (Internal & External Desires)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 33 of 161 --

Storytelling Made Simple:
1) Backstory (Internal & External Desires)
2) Problem With Current Way of Life
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 34 of 161 --

Storytelling Made Simple:
1) Backstory (Internal & External Desires)
2) Problem With Current Way of Life
3) Epiphany / New Information / Breaking Point
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 35 of 161 --

Storytelling Made Simple:
1) Backstory (Internal & External Desires)
2) Problem With Current Way of Life
3) Epiphany / New Information / Breaking Point
4) Conflict
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 36 of 161 --

Storytelling Made Simple:
1) Backstory (Internal & External Desires)
2) Problem With Current Way of Life
3) Epiphany / New Information / Breaking Point
4) Conflict
5) Overcome
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 37 of 161 --

Storytelling Made Simple:
1) Backstory (Internal & External Desires)
2) Problem With Current Way of Life
3) Epiphany / New Information / Breaking Point
4) Conflict
5) Overcome
6) Transformation (Internal) & Achievement (External)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 38 of 161 --

Example: Ham & Garlic
1) Backstory Boy loves dog. Wants dog happy. Wants
to be a responsible kid to make dad proud.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 39 of 161 --

Example: Ham & Garlic
1) Backstory Boy loves dog. Wants dog happy. Wants
to be a responsible kid to make dad proud.
2) Problem Dog has ticks.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 40 of 161 --

Example: Ham & Garlic
1) Backstory Boy loves dog. Wants dog happy. Wants
to be a responsible kid to make dad proud.
2) Problem Dog has ticks.
3) Epiphany Need to give garlic to get ticks off
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 41 of 161 --

Example: Ham & Garlic
1) Backstory Boy loves dog. Wants dog happy. Wants
to be a responsible kid to make dad proud.
2) Problem Dog has ticks.
3) Epiphany Need to give garlic to get ticks off
4) Conflict Dog wont eat garlic
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 42 of 161 --

Example: Ham & Garlic
1) Backstory Boy loves dog. Wants dog happy. Wants
to be a responsible kid to make dad proud.
2) Problem Dog has ticks.
3) Epiphany Need to give garlic to get ticks off
4) Conflict Dog wont eat garlic
5) Overcome Wrap ham in garlic
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 43 of 161 --

Example: Ham & Garlic
1) Backstory Boy loves dog. Wants dog happy. Wants
to be a responsible kid to make dad proud.
2) Problem Dog has ticks.
3) Epiphany Need to give garlic to get ticks off
4) Conflict Dog wont eat garlic
5) Overcome Wrap ham in garlic
6) Transformation
a) (Internal) : I am a responsible kid who will make
his dad proud for taking care of things.
b) Achievement (External): Dog is tick free
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

use simple stories to build a
WEAPONIZED COMMUNITY
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 48 of 161 --

Weaponized Community
Components:
I. Charismatic Leader
II. Future Based Cause
III. Unique Vocabulary
IV. Indoctrination Process
V. Uniform
VI. New Identity
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 49 of 161 --

Charismatic Leader
1) Evolution/Origins:
a) Baltimore
b) Consulting Gig
c) Drove Across Country
d) Started Gym, Slept on floor
e) 1 Gym grew to 6
f) Meet girl on internet
g) Mom got sick
h) Launched friends gym
i) Fly out to 30 cities 18 months
j) Teaching to fish instead of
DFY
k) 0 to $50M in 22 months
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 50 of 161 --

Charismatic Leader
1) Evolution/Origins
2) Dogma (IM GOING TO READ THESE TO YOU)
a) You don’t need new you need to get better at
fundamentals...daily listening to Sales Training 2-3x close rate.
Concept of a product. Cost of time to get value of benefit.
b) "how good you are and how much you get paid are unrelated"
c) Value Discrepancy is Secret To Retention/Business...you always
get paid for the thing you sold first, second
d) Launch Model For Recurring Revenue
e) Confront the Whole Price Up Front (whenever possible)
f) Payment Options/Terms Are Half The Battle
g) High Ticket Acquisition (Sell Highest Price When Most Excited)
h) Downsell Your Upsell
i) Closer Formula
j) Belief Breaking Formula
k) Chase Time Not Money
l) People Need To Be Sold To Be Served
a) No one values what they dont pay for. The more they pay the
more they value it.--single mom free challenge
b) Pain is the Pitch. If they cry they buy.
c) Client Financed Acquisition
d) Sell the cure.
e) Sell the vacation not the planeflight
f) Speed is King (for contacting leads)
g) Speed is king for implementation (your speed is how you win)
h) Agree on price, then agree on terms
i) Overwhelming social proof
j) 80% of people want to buy if you help them convince
themselves
k) #1 predictor of success is conviction
l) The Set is the close
m) # of reach outs is directly correlated with amount of sales
made---LEILA PHONE STORY
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 51 of 161 --

Charismatic Leader
1) Evolution/Origins
2) Dogma
a) A sale is always made, either you sell them on your conviction, or
they sell you on their excuse.
b) Broke people are easier to sell because they part with their
money more easily.
c) Rich people are easier to sell because they have lots of money
so its easy for them to part with a little of it.
d) Fulfill like crazy to sell as hard as you want
e) Never rest on your laurels
f) Boring makes you money
g) Most people suck at everything they do.
h) Be the most expensive
i) Jump rope story w Josh
j) Profit over revenue
k) Never copy--because everyone else is clueless
l) Abundant exchange
a) Help enough people get what they want to get what you want
b) Be obsessed w your customers
c) Attention is your most valuable asset. One business one focus.
RB + my blow up story.
d) Be ugly and make money. Branding doesnt matter.
e) Rip Cash out of your business
f) Lead by example, never blame employees/spouse---pastor
rocks sidewalk story
g) No crazies
h) authoritative prescription to sell
i) To scale service you scale people, which is culture &
training/onboarding/comm cycles
j) Teach people to fish, dont fish for them
k) Absolute profit over ROI %
l) Love education, hate formal education
m) Virtuous Cycle of Price, Help, Results
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 52 of 161 --

Charismatic Leader
1) Evolution/Origins
2) Dogma
a) Focus on your clients not the competition
b) # gyms in a market working with us vs # of gyms in a market PIE
c) 5 Core Functions of a Business
d) 3 Ways to Make More Money Acquire, Ascend, Resell
e) Rapid Results for Psychological Buy-in...Macros Story Nelly
f) Make Customers Not Sales
g) Accomodating Strength Curve & Value Ladder Comparison 80/20
Fractal Customers for Higher CPA
h) Irresistible Offers: benefit headline, splinter + bonuses, urgency
time frame, scarcity X spots, guarantee
i) Dual Marketing Calendar
j) Results dont matter, experience matters
k) Come for bikini, stay for community
l) One person in the angry boat
m) Business is like a garden not a machine
a) TWO CUSTOMERS: Ones who give you money and ones you
give money to. Market & Sell Employees just like you do
customers
b) Constantly sell employees
c) Must have an employee funnel
d) Tight Comm Cycles
e) Stack Cash
f) Riches in Niches--time mgmt story
g) LBOs Are BAD
h) People Buy Your Conviction/Certainty
i) Build Authority (Certs, Newspaper, Proof/testimonials, Celebrity
Endorsements, Pics on Stage).
j) People want to believe. They wan to be sold.
k) Become A Legend/Myth
l) Champions hate losing
m) Supply vs demand pricing (real inflation & margin)
n) Principles
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 53 of 161 --

Charismatic Leader
1) Evolution/Origins
2) Dogma
a) You can buy time by buying information
b) No regrets on soundly made decisions
c) FALSE "Two paychecks are better than one"
d) Sometimes the best clients are the ones never signed up.
e) Live week to week in exchange. Dont rest on laurels.
f) Do not tell yourself anything will be hard.
g) Make it easy for people to give you money--farmers market &
cash
h) Need something else to pull you if you want to make a lot
i) "Only a real business doesnt need you" FALSE
j) Explain false gym money math (500 @ 167/mo FALSE) Need
ladder
k) The restaurant failed because the food sucked
l) Closers ask hard questions/humor/rapport
a) Always record your sales--game footage--copy your swing when
youre on fire
b) Mechanic Price Overcome/Secretary Price Overcome
c) Get paid what youre worth as a coach...by coaching your
employees
d) PREFRAME MAGIC "Did you hear about us from Oprah?"
e) Sales is a consistent story line being reinforced or broken down
one step at a time
f) Have a script for normal comms in your biz
g) Best case worst case close/guarantees
h) Cheapest or Best, everyone else competes on "value"
i) Abundance in understanding the size of every market
j) Every single person who signs up for a gym responds to
marketing
k) People pay for access, accountability, and to be first,
information last.
l) "A customer born every minute"
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 54 of 161 --

Charismatic Leader
1) Evolution/Origins
2) Dogma
a) "Scared money dont make money"--FHTV example
b) Hair Dresser story---booked for 2 months, but i can take you
tomorrow. Send pic of part they hate.
c) GENIUS: Signing waiver for an extreme benefit "Erection lasting
longer than 4 hours please contact professional"
d) Best persuasion is persuasion that gos unnoticed
e) Minimum package, Standard package, assumed closes rental car
insurance story
f) The power of the word BUT
g) Damaging Admissions & Sacrificial lambs
h) BREAK Product--Price LINK Uneducated customers purchase on
price, must inform them of the difference
i) 3 Price Chains: buyer + timing, seller (who you are and how you
see yourself), context under which sold
j) Obstacle redirect (oxygen bar girl in vegas)
k) Burn Rate Equation/Pie Equation
a) Expressed interest
b) Group Sale Mess Up Story
c) Solve problems...there will always be more
d) Which tough conversation would you rather have (tough gym
money margin math)
e) Hustle in autoglass 20x any of his reps
f) Referrals on the contract for initiaion fee waive
g) Fame is a price strategy
h) The exact 300 workout that gerard butler
did...mythical...celebrity pricing
i) Sell status with your widget...pride of ownership, bragginr rights
& story to tell, makes it ok to spend so much since its cheaper
than other celebrity alternative
j) 4min Mile...my guys outsold everyone because they didnt know
any better
k) 5 Core functions Inside & Outside the business
l) Fear of losing it all fuels me...remember what is was like...so I
don’t spend on stupid stuff
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 55 of 161 --

Charismatic Leader
1) Evolution/Origins
2) Dogma
3) Exceptional
a) MUSTACHE
b) Harvard GMAT Score
c) 4000 1on1 closes
d) 6 gyms by 27 all off cash flow
e) Opened every facility at full cap before opening (off of pre-sale
cash)
f) 0 to $10M in 10months, 0 to $50M in 21 Months
g) 30 cities in 18 months traveling gym to gym to sell
h) IC Member of the year/Dream Car winner/First Person to Get 3 2
Comma + 1 X Award in First Year/Only Brick & Mortar Owner to
Speak Ever
i) Read every book by Dan Kennedy
j) Found wife with a funnel
a) Great shape physically--state record holder (powerlifting)--how you
do one thing everything
b) $175,000 in 45min pitch
c) Married in 8 Days--decisions dont take time, take info, Fast
Decisions
d) Came back from 0 and didnt file BK, bank screenshots
e) Never had a business Plan
f) $13,000/hr billable rate
g) Turned down two 50k for a day offers
h) Responsible for almost 750M in gym revenue
i) Highest Paid Microgym Consultant In the World
j) Brought 200 people into a store off of a campaign that took 85min to
make beginning to end
k) Top Secret Security Clearance at Age 21--Space, Cyber,
Intelligence
l) 81 for 84 on 100k sale on first call\
m) 3 languages fluently
n) President of Fraternity--men work off respect--so do
employees--outwork 552 transactions at chipotle--creature of habit
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Expectations

Product/Service

Charismatic Leader
1) Evolution/Origins
2) Dogma
3) Exceptional
4) Enemies
5) Flawed
a) 7 Failed Partnerships
b) Almost went bankrupt
c) DUI head on collision
d) Used to drink a half a bottle of black
label every night
e) Great memory for knowledge &
names, none for events
a) Disorganized, no details
b) Obsessive--forgetting to eat, sleep,
shower, lost 22lbs when leila left
for a month, eating pizza daily
c) Used to be harsh--clients running
past me in the lobby
d) Useless for most home projects

-- 59 of 161 --

Charismatic Leader
1) Evolution/Origins
2) Dogma
3) Exceptional
4) Enemies
5) Flawed
6) Language
7) Epiphany Stories
a) Sephora Story--Identity and priorities
b) Broke people arent good w money
c) Different wallets (like gma different stomachs)
d) Chocolate or Vanilla...assumed closes
e) Selling is serving. Must sell everyone all the time
f) Guarantees to never lose
g) Fritos on couch spouse close
h) Holiday overcome (realization about solving problem NOT
starting program)
i) Unicorn Close
j) Confront Fears Close--best case/worst case
a) Jump Rope Story
b) Business is garden not machine
c) Burger fries and shake profit maximizes for fitness services and
supps
d) when people eat at a buffet, the more choices they are given the
more they eat overall...sort of like selling multiple things to
people...the more they spend over all
e) Sell the vacation not the planeflight
f) Ham & the Garlic
g) Marbles & attention
h) Business is like a garden not a machine
i) 4min Mile...my guys outsold everyone because they didnt know
any better
j) Minimum package, Standard package, assumed closes rental car
insurance story
k) GENIUS: Signing waiver for an extreme benefit "Erection lasting
longer than 4 hours pleaes contact professional"
i) -->power of the word but
ii) -->Damaging admissions
l) Mechanic Price Overcome/Secretary Price Overcome

DOWN WHAT YOU TRULY
BELIEVE.

-- 75 of 161 --

Especially what people will
disagree with you about.

-- 76 of 161 --

“Helping mankind”...is not a
belief that is valuable from a
storytelling standpoint.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 77 of 161 --

But being AGAINST work-life
balance is.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 78 of 161 --

So you need to have courage to BE
YOU. For real.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 79 of 161 --

like you
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 80 of 161 --

So you REPEL those who are
unlike you

-- 91 of 161 --

Future Based Cause
You need to be able to articulate what your future
based cause is. Something your clients would
ACTUALLY get behind.
And YOU have to walk it out, or it will be words on a
wall.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 92 of 161 --

Future Based Cause

-- 100 of 161 --

Unique Vocabulary
1) Used to describe phenomena unique to the group
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 101 of 161 --

Unique Vocabulary
1) Used to describe phenomena unique to the group
2) This creates in-group / out-group bias
a) Which further solidifies the group
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 102 of 161 --

1) Used to describe phenomena unique to the group
2) This creates in-group / out-group bias
a) Which further solidifies the group
3) Shared context
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

2) This creates in-group / out-group bias
a) Which further solidifies the group
3) Shared context
4) More efficient communication, which increases in-group
affinity.

a) Which further solidifies the group

affinity.
a) Affinity means how much you LIKE something

-- 105 of 161 --

Unique Vocabulary
1) Used to describe phenomena unique to the group
2) This creates in-group / out-group bias
a) Which further solidifies the group
3) Shared context
4) More efficient communication, which increases in-group
affinity.
a) Affinity means how much you LIKE something
5) Being specific about language also standardizes
experience with clients (from an internal perspective)
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Unique Vocabulary
1) Language
a) Client Financed Acquisition
b) Abandon Hope Sale
c) Singe Moments
d) EFTs
e) Gym Lords
f) ALN Automated Lead Nurture
g) HTP High Ticket Program
h) Icing & Cake, Wedding Cake
Gym Model
i) Internal Plays/External Plays
j) Wall of Proof
k) Green Play
a) Cost Per lead
b) CPA cost of
acquisition
c) LTV Lifetime
Value
d) Ponton Passport
e) Splinter Offers
f) Profit Levers
g) Downsell the
Upsell

-- 107 of 161 --

Unique Vocabulary
What words or processes do you have in your community?
“The dug out”
“The pit”
“His & byes”
HINT: Name PLACES, PROCESSES, EVENTS, PROJECTS
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 108 of 161 --

Unique Vocabulary
Have noticed that I “name” every play we release?
Its because it gives more and more in-group/out-group bias.
And improves communication efficiency.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 109 of 161 --

Unique Vocabulary
NAME WHAT YOU DO.
NAME HOW YOU DO IT.
NAME WHERE EVERYONE WORKS.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Ad Case
Study
Success

Results Downsell
Upsell
Packages 	Reach
Outs
Releases/
Internal Play
Events 	On-Going
Support
Stories
Reinforced

New Identities Help People Adopt
NEW Belief Patterns FASTER
● World Class Service
● Profit > Revenue
● Garden Not Machine
● Team/Employees First, Clients Second
● High Price = High Service = Helping People
● Selling is Serving
● Higher Worth
● CERTAINTY > Confidence
● Community of Believers in A Better Future
● Hope
● ...and all the other DOGMAs you saw earlier
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 144 of 161 --

So what to do now...
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 145 of 161 --

So what to do now...
1) Document Your Stories, then tell them. Daily.
-->To your team
-->To your clients
2) Talk about what you BELIEVE, then mention
fitness.
3) Talk about things that people can strongly
disagree about with you.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com
